description: Operating system embodiment contract. Provides typed perception of OS state (screen, focused window, filesystem tree, process list, working directory, git state), typed action execution (shell commands, file I/O, keyboard/mouse/screen input), affordance enumeration over currently-available OS operations, and deterministic state hashing. The canonical `&body.*` subtype for agents that live on a machine.

perceive.description: Capture current OS state as a structured observation. Modes: 'screen' (screenshot + focused window), 'filesystem' (cwd + tree), 'process' (process list), 'shell' (env + history), 'git' (repo state), 'full' (all of the above). Sensitive: observations may include file contents, open windows, and environment variables; provider MUST honor &govern.identity scoping.

act.description: Execute a single typed OS action. Action types: shell_exec (shell command with stdin/stdout/stderr capture), file_read, file_write, file_edit, file_delete, keyboard_input (text or keystrokes), mouse_click (coordinate), mouse_drag, screen_capture, process_spawn, process_signal. Destructive actions (write/delete/spawn/signal) require &govern.* authorization; unauthorized attempts emit an escalation signal and return a typed error without execution.

affordances.description: Enumerate the typed actions currently available in the present OS state, optionally scoped to a path, window, or process. Returns an action_set describing what commands are installed, which files are writable under current permissions, which windows can be focused, etc. Affordances are policy-filtered: actions the agent is not authorized to take do not appear in the set.

encode_state.description: Produce a deterministic hash of the current OS state (cwd + focused_window_title + git_head + key_process_list). Used as FSM state identity for replay. Excludes volatile fields (timestamps, pids) by design.

replay.description: Execute a stored InteractionTrace against the current OS. Trace conforms to the OS-011 InteractionTrace schema. Replay fails fast on state_hash divergence (OS environments drift more than browsers, so strictness is higher). Destructive operations in the trace require re-authorization at replay time; trace authorization does not carry over.

metadata.authorization_required: &govern.identity must bind a valid agent_id; destructive actions require &govern.escalation confirmation per Delegatic policy
reference_implementations: Claude Code Bash + Read + Write + Edit tools (bounded)
example_use_cases: OS FSM recording + replay (competing with OpenClaw)
cross-machine skill transfer: one agent records a deploy workflow, another replays it
dark-factory autonomous operation with PRISM-benchmarked reliability
metadata.safety_notes: OS actions have wider blast radius than browser actions. Conforming providers MUST default-deny destructive operations without explicit &govern.* authorization. Replay of destructive traces MUST re-authorize at replay time, not inherit original authorization.
